notes: European standards body for telecommunications. Many ETSI deliverables (EN, TS, TR, GS) are FREE to download from etsi.org, though ETSI retains copyright and explicit terms govern reproduction. Notable security-relevant deliverables: EN 303 645 (consumer IoT cyber baseline), TS 103 645, EN 304 223 / TS 104 223 (baseline cyber security requirements for AI, from TC SAI), the ETSI TC CYBER series. License posture similar to ITU-T — flag for verification before redistributing.

match_nodes.description: ETSI EN 303 645 — Cyber Security for Consumer IoT

data.official_name: ETSI EN 303 645 - Cyber Security for Consumer Internet of Things: Baseline Requirements
data.notes: Free to download. Influential consumer-IoT cyber baseline; cited by NIST IR 8425 and various national IoT security labelling schemes.
ETSI Technical Committee CYBER publishes a wide range of cybersecurity standards including EN 303 645 (consumer IoT), TS 102 165 (threat/risk analysis), and various encryption profiles.

match_nodes.description: ETSI ISG QSC — Quantum-Safe Cryptography deliverables

data.official_name: ETSI Industry Specification Group on Quantum-Safe Cryptography (ISG QSC) deliverables
data.notes: Free download from etsi.org with ETSI copyright. ISG QSC publishes TS, TR, and GS deliverables covering post-quantum cryptography migration, hybrid key exchange, and quantum-safe TLS/IPsec profiles. Notable: TS 103 744 (Quantum-safe Hybrid Key Exchanges).

match_nodes.description: ETSI EN 304 223 / TS 104 223 — Securing AI: Baseline Cyber Security Requirements for AI Models and Systems

data.official_name: ETSI EN 304 223 - Securing Artificial Intelligence (SAI); Baseline Cyber Security Requirements for AI Models and Systems
data.notes: AI-security (treated as a subset of cybersecurity) baseline for AI models and systems: ~13 core principles expanding to roughly 72 provisions across 5 lifecycle phases (secure design, secure development, secure deployment, secure maintenance, secure end of life), covering AI-specific threats such as prompt injection, data poisoning, and model obfuscation. Provisions use citable identifiers of the form 'Provision X.Y.Z-N' (e.g., Provision 5.2.2-4 mandates a published vulnerability disclosure policy). First published as TS 104 223 v1.1.1 (April 2025), then adopted as European Standard EN 304 223 v2.1.1 (December 2025). Work item REN/SAI-0022. CAVEAT: ETSI deliverable PDFs were access-gated during research; the principle/provision counts and the 'Provision X.Y.Z-N' grammar are corroborated via ETSI newsroom + NCSC, not read verbatim from the standard — verify provision numbering before keying subpaths on it.
